title: Chairing a Divided Panel
description: Four panelists who disagree, one moderator who must keep the room together.
summary_pt_br: Moderando painel dividido: nomeie, aloque tempo, conduza ao ponto central, proteja silencios, feche com pergunta.

scenes.subtitle_en: Four panelists who disagree, one moderator who must keep the room together.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Moderando painel dividido: nomeie, aloque tempo, conduza ao ponto central, proteja silencios, feche com pergunta.

scenes.text_en: Four panelists who disagree, one moderator who must keep the room together. Focus: chairing a divided panel. I'm hearing two very different readings of the evidence — let's name that.
scenes.text_pt: Moderando painel dividido: nomeie, aloque tempo, conduza ao ponto central, proteja silencios, feche com pergunta. Foco: chairing a divided panel. Ouço duas leituras muito diferentes — vamos nomear.

scenes.title: Chairing a Divided Panel
scenes.body_en: Moves: SURFACE the disagreement (name it). ALLOCATE time (60 sec each). STEER toward the crux. PROTECT quieter voices. CLOSE with a question, not a conclusion.
scenes.body_pt: Moderando painel dividido: nomeie, aloque tempo, conduza ao ponto central, proteja silencios, feche com pergunta.

examples.en: I'm hearing two very different readings of the evidence — let's name that.
examples.pt: Ouço duas leituras muito diferentes — vamos nomear.

examples.en: Sixty seconds each, starting with the most recent objection.
examples.pt: 60 segundos cada, começando pela objeção mais recente.

examples.en: What question would we need to answer to move past this disagreement?
examples.pt: Que pergunta precisaríamos responder pra avançar?

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Chairing a Divided Panel

exercise.question: Complete: "I'm hearing two very ___ readings of the evidence — let's name that."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "I'm hearing two very different readings of the evidence — let's name that."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ouço duas leituras muito diferentes — vamos nomear.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Sixty seconds each, starting with the most recent objection.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Sixty seconds each, starting with the most recent objection."
exercise.hint_pt_br: 60 segundos cada, começando pela objeção mais recente.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Chairing a Divided Panel — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre chairing a divided panel. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Zara — four panelists, two loud, two silent. Open.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I hear two different readings. Let's name that.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: One panelist dominates for two minutes.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Anna, Carlos — I want your read next.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: What question would resolve this for everyone?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I leave you with one open question.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Chairing is the quietest leadership in the room.

scenes.title: The fractured panel
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe four moderation moves. Use at least three moderation terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At the annual conference, Zara chaired a panel where four panelists had been disagreeing publicly for weeks on social media.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: I'm hearing two very different readings of the evidence tonight — let's name that honestly before we continue.

paragraphs.speaker: Panelist A
paragraphs.en: The data is clear — any other reading is either dishonest or deeply uninformed about the underlying methodology.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Sixty seconds each from here, starting with Anna and Carlos — whose voices I haven't heard enough tonight yet.

paragraphs.speaker: Panelist B
paragraphs.en: What question would we need to answer to move past this disagreement, instead of restating it at higher volume?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Chairing a divided room, Zara believed, is the quietest form of leadership — invisible when it works, obvious when it fails.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Chairing a Divided Panel". Four panelists who disagree, one moderator who must keep the room together. I'm hearing two very different readings of the evidence — let's name that. Sixty seconds each, starting with the most recent objection. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Chairing a Divided Panel". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Chairing a Divided Panel".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Chairing a Divided Panel".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
